explanation: IB2-116 requires that the Image Widget can be resized via the Width slider in the Nav Panel (“Be able to set the ratio of the Image to change the ‘Block'’s width ... from Nav Panel”) and also retains direct-drag resizing. IB2-308 explicitly forbids changing Image Widget size from the Nav Panel slider and mandates resizing only via direct dragging (“The Image Widget's width must not be changeable from the Nav Panel Width slider” and “Any existing Width slider value in Nav should be read-only and should not update the Image Widget size”). These are mutually exclusive behaviors for how Image Widget width should be controlled.
suggested_fix: Clarify and reconcile the intended UX: either (A) adopt IB2-116 (allow Nav Width slider to change Image Widget width) and update IB2-308 to remove the 'must not' language, defining how slider and direct-drag interact (which is authoritative, how values sync, and expected undo/redo behavior), or (B) adopt IB2-308 (Nav slider read-only) and update IB2-116 to remove the Nav-resize requirement and document read-only behavior. Update acceptance criteria to state the single source of truth, include example flows (slider -> block update, drag -> slider update or not), and coordinate with related stories (undo/redo, responsive behavior) to ensure consistent behavior across the builder.

explanation: IB2-117 requires that the image 'be able to re-position and see the Image in the same percentage of ‘Block’ after resizing ... and the percentage will obtain if the 'Block' width changes', i.e. preserve proportional horizontal position. IB2-308 explicitly requires that when the Image Widget is resized, 'the image should reset to the center of its Block instead of preserving the same horizontal percentage position.' These are mutually exclusive behaviours for the same Image Widget UX.
suggested_fix: Product must choose one canonical behaviour (preserve proportional position vs. reset-to-center) and update both stories accordingly. If both behaviours are required in different contexts, specify the exact conditions/flags/roles that determine which behaviour applies (e.g., different widget modes or a user preference) and document them in acceptance criteria.

explanation: IB2-94 requires that when 'All users' is selected in a Workflow Template, "All Active platform users can be selected as a Collaborator" and that bypass permissions "will be extended to any selected user, even if they lack permission to the source item module." IB2-120 states that the new assign-collaborator action in the Approvals List displays only for users "who can see that request" and specifically calls out Requester and Reviewer roles. These statements contradict each other about whether collaborators may be users who do not currently have visibility/permission on the request (IB2-94 allows selecting such users and granting bypass; IB2-120 implies collaborator assignment is tied to users who already can see the request). Implementing both as written is logically inconsistent for the same permission/visibility behavior.
suggested_fix: Clarify the intended permission model in the Workflow Template: either (A) selecting 'All users' should allow selecting any active user and explicitly grant them the required access (and update IB2-120 to ensure the UI action and visibility reflect this), or (B) selecting collaborators must be limited to users who already have visibility on the request (and update IB2-94 to remove/soften the 'bypass permissions' claim). Update acceptance criteria and add a security note describing whether collaborator selection grants temporary access, how it propagates, and audit controls.

explanation: IB2-158 requires allowing a new 'Session' frequency option in My Account > Alerts (Markup) and explicitly states it should be selectable together with Hourly/Daily/Weekly. IB2-479 describes an Admin-level control for New Comments in Admin > Alert (Markup) that can 'turn on and apply only to daily, never' — which implies an admin can force ‘daily only’ for New Comments. If the admin-enforced 'daily only' setting prevents users from selecting 'Session', the two stories are mutually exclusive regarding availability of the 'Session' option for New Comments alerts.
suggested_fix: Clarify and document precedence between Admin-level alert constraints and My Account user preferences. Options: (A) specify that Admin 'apply only to daily' is a global override that disables the Session option in the user UI (update IB2-158 acceptance criteria to show disabled/hidden Session when overridden); or (B) specify Admin settings as defaults only (users may still select Session) and update IB2-479 to state it doesn't remove user-visible options. Update both stories' acceptance criteria to state expected behavior when an Admin restriction exists and add UI states for a restricted option (disabled with tooltip explaining admin enforcement).

explanation: IB2-164 requires the Drag & Drop Page limit to be available "in the Restrictions tab in MCP or Custom Page" (explicitly allowing a setting location in Custom Page). IB2-389 states the Page limits must be set "via MCP settings, not in Custom Page" (explicitly forbidding Custom Page). These are mutually exclusive requirements about the same configuration location for page limits.
suggested_fix: Clarify the single authoritative location and behavior for Page limits. Options: (a) Decide that MCP Restrictions tab is the only supported control (update IB2-164 to remove Custom Page placement), or (b) allow both MCP and Custom Page with a clear precedence/replication rule (update IB2-389 to permit Custom Page or describe synchronization). Update acceptance criteria in both stories to reflect the chosen approach and remove ambiguity or duplicate work.

explanation: IB2-78 requires that when deleting a Server fails 'the dialog remains open(?) and a snackbar appears with the error message'. IB2-172 explicitly requires that when deletion fails 'the confirmation dialog must close automatically' and 'No snackbar error message should be displayed after a failed delete action'. These are directly contradictory requirements for the SAME action (failed Server delete): one mandates keeping the dialog open and showing a snackbar, the other mandates closing the dialog and showing no snackbar.
suggested_fix: Clarify and unify the UX for failed Server deletes. Options: (A) Adopt IB2-78 behavior — keep the confirmation dialog open and show a snackbar error (update IB2-172 acceptance criteria to match), or (B) Adopt IB2-172 behavior — close the dialog and surface the error elsewhere (update IB2-78). Update both stories' acceptance criteria to state the chosen behaviour precisely (dialog open/closed, where error is shown, and any follow-up actions). Add a single cross-cutting UI error-handling guideline for Server CRUD operations to avoid future contradictions.

explanation: IB2-189 requires that the image position within its Block remain proportional after resizing to a smaller size and that proportional placement is preserved if the Block width later changes. IB2-308 explicitly requires that when the Image Widget is resized, the image should reset to the center of its Block instead of preserving the same horizontal percentage position. These are mutually exclusive behaviors for the same feature (image positioning after resize).
suggested_fix: Clarify the intended canonical behavior for image repositioning after resize: either preserve proportional placement across block width changes (IB2-189) or reset to center on resize (IB2-308). Update one or both stories to specify the authoritative rule (including any role-based or mode-based exceptions, e.g., a toggle setting) and ensure acceptance criteria reference the same behavior. If both behaviors are needed in different contexts, document the contexts and add conditional logic to the stories.

explanation: IB2-190 requires that after resizing/repositioning the Image (by dragging) the same horizontal percentage position of the Image within its Block is preserved across mobile/tablet/desktop responsive modes. IB2-308 explicitly requires that when the Image Widget is resized, the image should reset to the center of its Block instead of preserving the same horizontal percentage position. These two requirements are mutually exclusive for the same behavior (image horizontal position after resize) and cannot both be true.
suggested_fix: Clarify and align the responsive-positioning requirement. Options: (a) choose to preserve the horizontal percentage across responsive modes (keep IB2-190 behavior) and remove the 'reset to center' clause from IB2-308; or (b) limit 'reset to center' to specific cases (e.g., when resized via Nav slider) and document that dragging preserves percentage across modes. Update acceptance criteria in both stories to state the authoritative behavior and any conditional exceptions.

explanation: IB2-27 defines the Workflow Request status values shown in the Approvals List column as: Pending, Approved, Declined, Reviewed, Cancelled. IB2-497 (V2 Database Settings - Feature Field Completion Value) lists available approval status options as: Approved, Cancelled, Completed, Declined, Pending. The two stories assert different canonical status value sets (IB2-27 uses 'Reviewed' while IB2-497 uses 'Completed'), which creates an inconsistent source-of-truth for approval/status semantics across the product.
suggested_fix: Align the canonical approval/status enum across both stories. Decide whether the canonical value is 'Reviewed' or 'Completed' (or map them explicitly), update both the Approvals List UI (IB2-27) and the Feature Field Completion Value options (IB2-497) to reference the same shared enum/constant, and add a single source of truth (documentation or central config) to prevent divergence. If both terms must exist for legacy reasons, add clear mappings and conversion rules and update acceptance criteria to document the mapping.

explanation: IB2-323 describes the Ask button for 'a user' (UI: button displays under question field, disabled by default, hover text 'Click or hit enter to ask your question.'), implying general user availability. IB2-326 explicitly restricts the Ask button to Main Admin users only and states it 'must only be displayed after the Alpha Main Admin access restriction has been implemented' (also repeats disabled-by-default and same hover text). The two stories therefore make mutually exclusive claims about Ask-button visibility/scope for the same UI element (general user vs Main Admin-only). Implementing both as-written is logically inconsistent.
suggested_fix: Clarify and consolidate the visibility and permission requirements. Options: (a) Convert IB2-323 into a generic UI-only story that explicitly states the role-scope (e.g., 'visible to any user with Ask AI permission') and mark IB2-326 as the permissions/availability story that implements the Main Admin restriction; or (b) Merge the two: make a single Ask-button story that includes role-based visibility/permission acceptance criteria (default disabled state, hover text, and 'display only for users with Alpha Main Admin access' or allow both roles but gated by feature flag). Ensure acceptance criteria explicitly reference the required permission checks and the dependency on 'AI Assist visibility rules' and 'Limit to current view' logic.

explanation: IB2-80 states that when no Master Templates exist the system displays a 'No Master Templates available' message and the user can select 'Create a Blank Template' instead. IB2-337 explicitly requires the opposite: 'Start with a blank template' must only be displayed when at least one Master Template exists and if none are available users must not be allowed to create a blank template and should instead be instructed to create a Master Template first. These are mutually exclusive UX/business rules for the exact same user action.
suggested_fix: Resolve with product: decide the desired behavior when no Master Templates exist (allow creating a blank template vs. require creating a Master Template first). Update the canonical design and both stories' acceptance criteria to match. If both behaviors must exist under different conditions (e.g., role-based or feature-flagged), document those conditions explicitly in each story and add acceptance tests for each path.

explanation: IB2-420 assumes mixed selections (some requests with Hide Decline enabled and some without) can occur in the Bulk Review flow and prescribes showing Decline while ignoring Bulk Apply of Decline for inapplicable items. IB2-455 states that once one pending request is selected, "only checkboxes for relevant related Pending requests remain selectable" — which implies the UI may restrict selection to only 'relevant' (homogeneous) requests and therefore prevent the mixed-selection scenario IB2-420 targets. The two stories make incompatible assumptions about whether mixed-parameter selections are possible in Bulk Review.
suggested_fix: Clarify the intended selection model in acceptance criteria: either (A) enforce homogeneous selection (update IB2-455 to explicitly state which parameters determine 'relevant' and that Hide Decline differences will be prevented), in which case IB2-420 should be adjusted or deprioritised; or (B) allow mixed selections (confirm IB2-420 behavior), and update IB2-455 to explicitly allow selecting items with differing Hide Decline settings and document how checkboxes and validation behave. Update both stories' AC to reference the same rule about Hide Decline and bulk-selection semantics and add UI examples to resolve ambiguity.

explanation: IB2-463 requires a mechanism to allow complete removal/wiping of existing facial data and related flags during MCP Site clone (e.g., a checkbox to wipe existing data). IB2-476 requires that existing Unknown face flags be preserved during clone handling so the “Unknown People” filter still returns matching image resources. These are mutually exclusive on the same topic (handling of face flags during clone): one mandates removal, the other mandates preservation.
suggested_fix: Clarify intended clone behavior in acceptance criteria: specify whether wiping should be optional (e.g., default preserve, with an explicit 'Wipe facial data' checkbox to remove flags) or mandatory. Update IB2-463 to state the checkbox semantics (default state, scope of wipe, whether it affects Unknown flags) and update IB2-476 to reference the checkbox behavior (e.g., 'if wipe is not selected, Unknown flags are preserved') so both stories can be implemented without contradiction.

explanation: IB2-117 and IB2-189 specify the same user-facing behaviour and UI details for repositioning an Image Widget by dragging the image: both require showing a visual outline + full widget boundary with 8px spacing after placing an Image Widget, keeping the outer border visible after resizing, and preserving the image's proportional position within the Block after resizing and when the Block width changes. The deliverables are effectively identical, so implementing one would make the other redundant.
suggested_fix: Consolidate IB2-189 into IB2-117 (or mark one as duplicate). Keep the canonical acceptance criteria in a single story and remove the duplicate to avoid duplicated engineering work.

explanation: IB2-190 and IB2-117 both describe repositioning the image by dragging the image itself and preserving the same horizontal percentage within the Block after resizing. IB2-190 adds explicit mention of 'mobile, tablet, and desktop modes' (responsive modes), while IB2-117 already references percentage retention when the Block width changes. The overlap is large and could result in duplicated implementation or unclear ownership of responsive behaviour unless clarified.
suggested_fix: Clarify scope: either merge IB2-190 into IB2-117 (making the responsive-mode acceptance criteria explicit in IB2-117), or delineate responsibilities (IB2-117 = core drag/reposition behavior; IB2-190 = verification across responsive breakpoints). Update acceptance criteria to state whether IB2-117 already includes responsive behaviour.

explanation: IB2-189 states the Image Widget should keep the image position proportional and preserve that proportional placement if the Block width changes. IB2-190 requires that the position and size set for the image still work in different responsive modes and that the same horizontal percentage of the image with the Block is retained after resizing and repositioning. Both stories require the same technical behavior (preserving horizontal percentage/proportional placement across responsive modes), so implementing IB2-189's proportional-preservation would largely satisfy IB2-190.
suggested_fix: Confirm whether IB2-190 is intended as a separate implementation workstream (responsive-mode testing/QA) or is covered by IB2-189. If redundant, consolidate acceptance criteria into one story (preferably keep the UI/visual details from IB2-189 and the cross-device verification from IB2-190) and close or convert the other into a dependent test/QA ticket.

explanation: Both stories specify the same UI behavior: reorder pages via drag-and-drop in the Navigation panel within the same parent/root, move child pages together with the parent, and collapse sub-pages under the parent after the move. The acceptance criteria are functionally equivalent; implementing IB2-215 would fully satisfy IB2-385.
suggested_fix: Consolidate the two tickets into a single implementation (merge acceptance criteria and tests). Close one story as a duplicate and ensure the combined story covers all edge cases (root-pages, sub-pages, collapse behavior, and any UI states).

explanation: IB2-225 (PB - create a sub-page via Nav) and IB2-415 (PB - create a sub-page via Nav) describe the same front-end feature: clicking the settings button in Nav, showing the sub-page popup, inputting the sub-page title and saving. The acceptance criteria overlap almost exactly (including label change 'Done' → 'Save'), so completing one PB story would largely produce the same deliverable and the other story would duplicate effort.
suggested_fix: Merge IB2-415 into IB2-225 (or vice versa). Consolidate all acceptance criteria and design references into the single PB story. If there are minor differences, add them as explicit acceptance-criteria or tasks/sub-tasks. Link any backend work (e.g., IB2-396) as a dependency rather than a duplicate.

explanation: IB2-429 has the same title and acceptance criteria as IB2-233: both specify the new extend/collapse button in the top-left to open the Left Side Panel as Page Nav, showing all saved Simple pages filtered by the current user's permissions. Implementing IB2-233 would fully deliver the functionality described in IB2-429.
suggested_fix: Consolidate the two stories into one canonical story (choose one key to keep) or remove IB2-429. If there are any minor differences intended, update the kept story's acceptance criteria to include those specifics so a single story fully captures the desired behavior.

explanation: IB2-240 and IB2-448 specify the same feature and acceptance criteria: both require the Bulk Apply experience for Grouped work to follow the Grouped Review page behavior (apply button item counts, hover tooltip text for enabled/disabled states, and snackbar notifications), to use the same apply processing logic, and to determine Complete Request/Stage actions based on whether requests are staged or standalone. Implementing one would make the other redundant.
suggested_fix: Consolidate IB2-240 and IB2-448 into a single story (or mark one as a duplicate). Merge and deduplicate acceptance criteria and owners so the team implements the Bulk Apply behavior once. If there are any distinct validation checks mentioned in either story, move them into clear sub-tasks of the merged story.

explanation: Both stories ask for the same user action: creating a sub-page from the navigation/settings flow and showing the sub-page popup. IB2-396: 'Be able to click on setting button of a simple page from Nav' + 'Be able to see the sub-page popup as per'. IB2-415: 'Be able to click on setting button of a simple page from Nav' + 'Be able to see the sub-page popup as per' and additionally 'Be able to input the sub page's title and save (change "Done" to "Save")'. The core technical work (opening the nav, handling settings click, displaying the sub-page popup) would be implemented twice if both are built separately.
suggested_fix: Consolidate into a single story or make one the canonical implementation with the extra acceptance criteria. Decide whether this applies to Simple Pages, Smart Pages, or both; unify the acceptance criteria to include the title input and the button-label change if required. If they must remain separate (different page types), clearly document the differences in scope and UI expectations to avoid duplicate engineering work.

explanation: Both stories require the bulk-grouped-work UI to match the existing Grouped Review behaviour and explicitly reference tooltip/button enabled/disabled states. IB2-447 requires the user to hover over the 'Submit & Continue' button and that a hover tooltip must appear before submission. IB2-448 mandates following the same Grouped Review behaviour, specifically calling out 'tooltip text for enabled and disabled button states' and parity of the Apply button experience. This overlap suggests the same UI affordance (hover/tooltip gating of a bulk action button in grouped work) may be implemented twice unless clarified.
suggested_fix: Clarify and consolidate scope: decide whether 'Submit & Continue' (IB2-447) and the 'Apply' action (IB2-448) are the same UI control/flow. If so, merge acceptance criteria into a single story or make one the canonical implementation story and convert the other to an integration/verification story. If they are distinct actions, update each story to explicitly differentiate the target control, page, and acceptance criteria (e.g., which button label, which page/state, and exact tooltip text and hover gating difference).

explanation: The story is phrased as a question/uncertainty: 'what exactly are the new widget settings...' — it does not define a concrete, testable outcome or acceptance criteria that the team can estimate against.
suggested_fix: Replace the question with explicit acceptance criteria listing each new merged setting for Block and Button widgets, expected defaults, UI locations, interactions, and any permission/validation rules so the team can estimate and implement.

explanation: The last acceptance criterion is truncated/unclear ('and the percentage will obtain if the 'Block' width changes by'), leaving the expected behavior after block-width changes undefined. The incomplete statement prevents reliable estimation.
suggested_fix: Complete and clarify the acceptance criteria: define expected percentage calculations, how repositioning is persisted, exact padding/box behavior after resize, and any edge cases (e.g., minimum/maximum sizes). Provide design reference or example scenarios.

explanation: Acceptance criteria are ambiguous about who sees and can perform the new 'assign collaborators' action (incomplete sentence about 'Other added collaborators'). Permission boundaries and exact actor list are not clearly defined, so the team cannot estimate responsibilities or necessary permission checks.
suggested_fix: Specify exact rules: which user roles/actors see the action, which users can perform assignments, how the Workflow Template setting gates the action, expected UI/location/text, and required backend permission checks. Add examples for staged vs non‑staged requests.

explanation: The description is vague and lacks concrete acceptance criteria — it only states a high‑level consensus (show logo, not login/nav menu) and a loose analogy to 'CPSL links'. There is no definition of which auth screens, success page content, responsive behavior, or navigation changes are required.
suggested_fix: Define clear acceptance criteria: list each screen to update (login, register, password reset, success page), exact UI components to display/hide (logo, menus, links), responsive rules, accessibility requirements, and screenshots or designs. Specify expected navigation state and any differences for various user roles or routes.

explanation: The story doesn't define what 'Drag & Drop Page limit' actually is (max items, characters, file size?), how it's configured (field type, min/max/default), where exactly it appears in MCP vs Custom Page, or expected validation/behaviour. Team cannot reliably estimate work from the current description.
suggested_fix: Clarify the exact measurable requirement: define what the 'limit' controls (e.g. max number of draggable blocks per page), allowed values and defaults, UI location and wording in Restrictions tab, expected validation and error messages, and whether changing the limit affects existing pages immediately or only new pages.

explanation: The story is incomplete/truncated: it only states combining Block and Widget settings accessible from Nav but doesn't list which settings, scope, expected UI layout, or behaviour for each widget. Without specifics the effort and acceptance are unclear.
suggested_fix: Provide a complete acceptance criteria list: enumerate which Block and Text Widget properties are included, intended UI grouping/labels, default values, permission rules, and expected behavior (save/preview/validation). Include mockups or a mapping of old settings → new combined UI.

explanation: The acceptance criteria contain contradictory and unclear statements (e.g. 'Module appears if it applies to User’s usage report, whilst in Custom Page usage report the Module will not be displayed') and an ambiguous admin config reference. The scope and exact reporting rules are not defined, so the team cannot estimate implementation.
suggested_fix: Clarify the reporting rules with concrete cases: specify when the 'Module' should be included/excluded, exact event schema for 'Resource Download (Preview)', how 'Custom Page' naming is derived from admin config, expected search/export columns, and sample expected rows. Add example queries/reports or a small decision table.

explanation: The story refers to multiple behaviours ('follow same as Grouped Review', 'actual apply logic', 'extra check') without a clear, self-contained definition of expected behaviour or edge cases. Dependence on unspecified Grouped Review behaviour and ambiguous rules for staged vs single requests prevents reliable estimation.
suggested_fix: Attach a precise spec or link to the canonical Grouped Review behaviour, and enumerate the expected behaviours in this context (button states, tooltip text, snackbar messages, exact apply logic and how Complete Request/Stage decisions are derived). Provide sample workflows showing staged vs single request outcomes and expected UI/DB state transitions.

explanation: The story says 'combine previously defined Block and Widget's settings' but does not enumerate which Block properties must be included or how they should behave in the Nav. Only two Button fields are listed, leaving the rest of the scope ambiguous. The team cannot confidently estimate effort without a complete list of settings and expected UI/behavior.
suggested_fix: List the exact settings to be exposed for both Block and Button widgets (full property names, types, default values, validation, and expected Nav-panel UI). Add explicit acceptance criteria showing the expected UI state before/after changes and any backward-compatibility rules.

explanation: The description is a fragment referencing multiple scenarios ('as detailed in scenarios above') without listing concrete acceptance criteria. It is unclear exactly how cross-folder edits should determine reviewer selection or email behavior in each case, so the team cannot reliably estimate effort.
suggested_fix: Provide complete, enumerated scenarios and expected outcomes for each cross-folder combination (e.g., Global+Local, Local+Local) including the exact email templates, reviewer resolution rules, and examples. Add acceptance criteria that map inputs (folders/templates/config) to the desired email/notification behavior.

explanation: The story requests 'set Page limits on MCP control panel for client sites' but does not define what limits (per-site total pages, per-user pages, page size, depth?), default values, validation rules, or how limits are enforced/displayed. Without this, the team cannot estimate implementation effort or UX impact.
suggested_fix: Specify the exact limits to be supported (type, units, scope: per-site/per-user), default and maximum values, expected MCP UI fields and permissions, enforcement behavior (soft warning or hard block), and API/DB changes. Add acceptance criteria showing the end-to-end flow for setting and enforcing a sample limit.

explanation: The description is incomplete and ambiguous (poor grammar, missing concrete acceptance criteria and references). It's unclear exactly what logic should change, which lists/components are affected, and the desired behavior for different user roles, so the team cannot reliably estimate implementation effort.
suggested_fix: Clarify the expected behavior with concrete acceptance criteria: show current vs desired examples (before/after), list the exact V2 components to exclude, specify who can re-assign in each case, and include edge cases. Link to any relevant design or workflow config docs.

explanation: The story references UI behavior using placeholders ('as per') with no attached design, expected fields, or acceptance criteria. Without the referenced popup design and exact fields/actions, the team cannot estimate the effort or define done.
suggested_fix: Attach or link the sub-page popup design and list expected fields/actions (defaults, validation, required permissions). Specify success criteria (e.g., modal opens from Nav settings, creates child page linked to parent, response codes, and any navigation/refresh behavior).

explanation: The story mixes vague conditional behavior ('may have some chances to appear') with a very broad non-functional wishlist (multilingual generation, real-time tracking, external synchronization). It's unclear which of these are in-scope for this story and which are future/architectural requirements, so effort cannot be estimated.
suggested_fix: Clarify scope: state which notification capabilities must be delivered in this story vs which are future aspirations. Replace vague language with concrete acceptance criteria (e.g., when setting X and Y are enabled, show option Z; implement template inheritance for tenant-level overrides; multilingual support limited to N languages initially). If broader integration items are needed, split into separate stories (e.g., tracking/integration).

explanation: The expected behavior is ambiguous and self-contradictory: title and description suggest different outcomes about when 'Decline' should be hidden or shown during Bulk Review for mixed-selection items. The story lacks clear rules for UI state and bulk-apply semantics.
suggested_fix: Define exact business rules with examples: specify when 'Decline' should be visible/disabled/hidden for mixed selections, how bulk-apply should ignore non-applicable items, and the expected user feedback. Provide acceptance criteria with sample item sets and expected UI/processing outcome.

explanation: The story asks to 'Add new below setting under Development Use Only Databases - Kanban Sort Options' but provides no detail about what options, behavior, or data model the setting controls. Without that, the team cannot estimate implementation or testing effort.
suggested_fix: Specify the setting name, allowed values, intended effect on Kanban sorting (e.g., fields affected, default behavior, user-visible consequences), placement in MCP UI, permission requirements, and any non-functional constraints (performance).

explanation: The story requests adding an MCP setting for 'Approvals List Bulk Actions Updates' but does not define what the setting enables/disables or the behavioral outcomes. The lack of scope/detail prevents confident estimation.
suggested_fix: Clarify what the new setting toggles (which bulk actions it affects), desired default state, who can change it, exact placement in MCP, and acceptance criteria showing before/after behavior examples.

explanation: The description uses uncertain language ('should probably', 'may show', 'may be hidden', 'may autoplay') and lacks definitive acceptance criteria for UX details (controls visibility, autoplay behavior, exact preview behavior). With those unknowns the team cannot reliably estimate effort or QA scope.
suggested_fix: Clarify exact expected behavior on the Share screen: whether the MP4 preview is mandatory, whether playback controls are shown, autoplay rules (desktop/mobile, muted/un-muted), looping, thumbnail fallback, and performance/size limits. Replace tentative language with concrete acceptance criteria and examples.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] IB2-111 (embed Block background colour setting into Text Widget toolbar) assumes the combined Block+Widget settings model described by IB2-186 exists. The toolbar embedding cannot be fully defined or acceptance-tested until the merged widget/block configuration surface is implemented.
suggested_fix: Deliver IB2-186 (combined Block/Text widget properties) first or deliver a clear contract/spec for the combined settings so IB2-111 can be implemented against it.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] IB2-190 (responsive behaviour preserving horizontal percentage after resizing/repositioning) depends on the core resizing and repositioning interactions being implemented (IB2-116 / IB2-117). The responsive preservation behaviour cannot be tested without those base interactions functioning.
suggested_fix: Implement/resolves IB2-116 and IB2-117 (resizing and repositioning interaction) before scheduling IB2-190, or include them in the same integrated work item with explicit acceptance criteria for responsive modes.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] IB2-191 (Undo/Redo for resizing & repositioning) requires the resizing and repositioning actions to exist and emit undoable events. Without IB2-116/IB2-117 implemented, Undo/Redo behaviour cannot be meaningfully implemented or validated.
suggested_fix: Deliver the core resize/reposition functionality first (IB2-116, IB2-117) and ensure they produce the action events/state needed for undo/redo; then implement IB2-191.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] IB2-308 (make Image Widget resizing controlled only by direct dragging and make Nav width slider read-only) presumes a Width slider currently exists in the Nav (as described in IB2-116). Changing the slider to read-only or altering its behaviour cannot be validated until the existing slider behaviour is present or stubbed.
suggested_fix: Schedule IB2-116 (Nav Width slider behaviour) prior to or together with IB2-308, or provide a temporary implementation of the slider to allow IB2-308 to alter its state to read-only for acceptance testing.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] IB2-225 (FE: create a sub-page via Nav) is a front-end interaction that requires a back-end endpoint to actually create and persist a sub-page. IB2-396 is the backend story for creating a sub-page; without it the FE cannot be meaningfully implemented, tested end-to-end, or accepted.
suggested_fix: Prioritise IB2-396 (or provide a backend mock/stub API) before or in the same sprint as IB2-225 to allow end-to-end testing and acceptance.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] IB2-245 (show Bulk Actions checkbox column when relevant) is explicitly gated by an MCP development setting and by a per-template setting. IB2-75 is the MCP setting story for Approvals List Bulk Actions; without that setting (or the template-level toggle) the column behaviour cannot be validated or delivered as described.
suggested_fix: Implement the MCP setting from IB2-75 (and document the template toggle) before implementing or testing IB2-245. If schedule requires parallel work, provide a feature-flag shim to allow FE testing.

explanation: [EXTREME_BOTTLENECK] IB2-465 (provide Resource module access via a lookup button for Page Builder) is a clear prerequisite for multiple Page Builder stories: IB2-436 (video resource selection into Video Widget), IB2-387 (download button for Image Widget resources), IB2-281 (link background image status with Resource module via Resource Lookup), and IB2-388 (permission-lost/resource-placeholder behaviour). If IB2-465 is delayed, all these FE features are blocked.
suggested_fix: Treat IB2-465 as a priority backend/integration deliverable. If it cannot be completed early, provide a stable mocked Resource Lookup API or a small contract-only shim so dependent FE stories can progress in parallel. Alternatively, decompose IB2-465 so core lookup and permission-check endpoints are available first.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] IB2-326 (Ask button behavior & scoping) explicitly states it must be implemented after the Alpha Main Admin visibility rules and the 'Limit to current view' search-scope logic exist. Without IB2-318 (Main Admin visibility) and IB2-299 (Limit-to-current-view checkbox / search-scope), IB2-326 cannot be tested or accepted as specified.
suggested_fix: Complete IB2-318 and IB2-299 first (or provide a test harness that enforces the same visibility/limit behaviour) so IB2-326 can be implemented and validated.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] IB2-9 (GIF/Lottie -> Share-screen preview) is explicitly gated by the MCP feature flag described in IB2-3. The preview feature 'must become enabled' only when the 'Resources - GIF/Lottie Preview Support' setting exists and is enabled, so IB2-9 cannot be meaningfully accepted or delivered until IB2-3 is implemented and the flag is available to toggle.
suggested_fix: Implement the MCP setting from IB2-3 (or provide a test-mode flag/mocks) before scheduling IB2-9. Alternatively, implement a behind-flag mock of preview behavior for acceptance tests until IB2-3 is available.

explanation: The acceptance criteria explicitly say this story must be implemented after 'AI Assist visibility rules' and the 'Limit to current view' logic are available. That is a hard dependency on other stories being completed first, so it cannot be developed/released independently.
suggested_fix: Either remove the hard sequencing and make the Ask-button story tolerant of missing pieces (feature-flagged/conditional behavior), or split work: (A) implement the Ask-button UI and local enable/disable behavior now, and (B) a follow-up story to integrate visibility and scope logic. Reference the dependent story keys in the DoR/implementation notes rather than blocking acceptance.

explanation: The story explicitly says the randomized confirmation should only be applied after the public upload page uses the simplified header state and 'should wait until IB-382 completed' — indicating a dependency on IB-382 being done first.
suggested_fix: Remove the blocking dependency or split the work: implement the simplified header (IB-382) first, or build the randomized presentation behind a feature flag so the success-state can be implemented and tested independently.

explanation: [NOT_INDEPENDENT] IB2-383 (move sub-page to standalone, migrate child references, update URLs, sync across APIs/mobile/exports) requires read/write Smart Page APIs and related endpoints. The Smart Page APIs (IB2-51: list, IB2-52: get details, IB2-53: delete) are prerequisites to implement and validate the cross-API synchronization and URL/breadcrumb/permission updates described.
suggested_fix: Deliver the Smart Page APIs (list/get/delete) and any required create/update endpoints, or provide comprehensive API contracts and test doubles, before implementing IB2-383. Plan coordinated FE+BE work and include migration scripts / API versioning in the schedule.

explanation: This story bundles multiple, distinct deliverables (UI setting, provider-switching logic, migration of Imagga configs and usage history, billing/quota changes, analytics/reporting, notifications, API/permissions updates, docs and rollout config). That scope is broad enough to be an epic and unlikely to be completed and validated in a single sprint.
suggested_fix: Split into smaller, independently deliverable stories (examples: 1) Add MCP setting & provider switch logic; 2) Migrate Imagga configs and usage history; 3) Billing and quota-tracking updates; 4) Analytics & provider-activity reports; 5) Notifications for limits/failures; 6) API/permissions and documentation; 7) Rollout/feature-flag config). Ensure each has clear acceptance criteria and testable DoD.

explanation: The story bundles many separate deliverables (schema change to include Q/A/Rating, renaming report, removing dev servers, historical data migration, multi‑environment aggregation, backward compatibility, automated export synchronization). This is a broad, cross‑cutting effort (including migration and integration work) and cannot reasonably be completed in a single sprint as written.
suggested_fix: Split into smaller stories: (a) add Q/A/Rating fields and update report schema + rename, (b) implement filtering to remove dev server entries, (c) implement/export changes for multi‑environment aggregation, (d) create & validate historical data migration script, and (e) implement automated export/synchronization. For each, include data volumes, target environments, and backward compatibility acceptance criteria.

explanation: The story bundles many large, distinct capabilities (drag-and-drop move, migrate child references and permissions, update URLs/breadcrumbs/SEO, undo/redo, full version history, audit logging, notifications, workflows, role-based permissions, cross-client synchronization). This is an epic-level scope, not a single story deliverable for one sprint.
suggested_fix: Split into multiple smaller stories that each deliver independent value. Example slices: (1) UI: allow moving a sub-page to top-level and persist navigation order, (2) Data migration: update child references and permissions, (3) Routing: update URLs/breadcrumbs/SEO, (4) UX: undo/redo and basic version history, (5) Ops/security: audit logging and notifications, (6) Sync/integration: API/mobile/exports sync. Define acceptance criteria for each slice.

explanation: The story bundles multiple substantial capabilities (view/configure sub-pages, permission propagation, hierarchical inheritance across multiple levels, approval workflows, audit history, and cross-platform synchronization). This is an epic-sized scope that cannot reasonably be completed and validated in a single sprint.
suggested_fix: Split into smaller stories/work items: e.g., (1) UI: list and basic edit of sub-page permissions, (2) implement hierarchical permission propagation core logic, (3) add multi-level propagation & inheritance rules, (4) approval workflow for permission changes, (5) audit/history recording, (6) cross-platform synchronization. For each child story provide acceptance criteria and success metrics.

explanation: The story requests the Page Category to 'deselect and reselect itself' (briefly disappear and reappear) every time a Page is viewed. That behavior appears to be arbitrary visual flicker and is likely user‑hostile or pointless rather than delivering a meaningful user or business benefit.
suggested_fix: Clarify the real user problem. If the intent is to refresh navigation state or ensure updated page lists, specify a non‑flickering approach (e.g., reapply selection without visual disappearance, animate a clear refresh indicator, or only refresh when underlying data changes). If this is describing a bug, convert to a bug report describing the undesired flicker and expected stable selection behavior.

explanation: The story's primary requested behavior is that integrated fields 'briefly disappear and reappear' whenever displayed. That visual flicker is an arbitrary UX behavior that adds confusion/friction without a stated user or business benefit. The acceptance text focuses on transient hiding rather than a useful functional outcome.
suggested_fix: Describe the user problem driving this behavior (e.g. refresh to pick up external data) and replace ‘briefly hide and show’ with a clear, user-friendly approach (e.g. show a loading skeleton/spinner or gracefully update values in-place). Specify timing, animation, and accessibility expectations so the team can implement a usable refresh experience instead of unexplained flicker.

explanation: The acceptance criteria instruct that on deletion failure the confirmation dialog must close and no snackbar error should be shown. Suppressing failure feedback hides important error information from users and prevents recovery or retry — an actively user-hostile behavior with no justification given.
suggested_fix: Rework the UX to surface meaningful error feedback on failure. Suggested alternative: keep or re-open the dialog and show a clear inline error message, and/or display a non-blocking snackbar with the failure reason and retry option. If hiding errors is required for a specific reason, record the justification and define what alternate feedback (audit log, retries, telemetry) will be provided.

explanation: The core requested outcome is arbitrary randomization of the submission success presentation (different tick sizes, randomized wording position/presentation). Randomizing confirmation UI on each submission is inconsistent, harms usability and accessibility, and does not provide a clear user or business benefit.
suggested_fix: Clarify the business rationale for variability. If the intent is to A/B test or add delight, define measurable goals and constraints (accessibility, consistency, analytics). Prefer a small set of approved, accessible variants with deterministic selection (A/B targeting) rather than random per-load changes.

explanation: The story's primary outcome is to require users to hover over a submit button before Bulk Submit is enabled. This introduces deliberate friction (hover requirement) without stated rationale or user-benefit. As written, it appears arbitrary and potentially user-hostile rather than delivering clear product/business value.
suggested_fix: Explain the user problem this hover requirement solves (e.g., accidental submissions) and consider safer alternatives (confirmation dialog, undo, two-step submit, clearer labeling). If hover is required for accessibility/consistency reasons, document the rationale and include acceptance criteria ensuring it meets accessibility standards. Otherwise, remove the hover requirement or replace it with a less intrusive safeguard.
